title: mentha longifolia subsp. lavandulacea
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. lavandulacea, also known as Woolly Mentha or Woolly Mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant characterized by its greyish-white, woolly or downy leaves. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it a popular choice for various herbal remedies. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subsp. lavandulacea include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Mentha longifolia subsp. lavandulacea is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, ease symptoms of colds and flu, and support overall well-being.
